steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must (1) quote Fazal, (2) explain an advantage of the system at Platanares specifically, and (3) connect that advantage to the binary cycle system Platanares uses. Fazal's relevant quote is: binary cycle systems "pump 100% geothermal fluid back to the injection well, hence offering the least environmental impact with little or no emissions."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Compared with dry steam systems and flash steam systems, the binary cycle system used at the Platanares plant is "capable of producing more energy per megawatt per year," Fazal notes.': Uses the wrong Fazal quote. The per-megawatt quote compares geothermal energy to solar and wind in general — it does not describe an advantage of the binary cycle system specifically. ✗
  • Choice B — 'According to Fazal, because it uses high-temperature geothermal fluid to produce electricity, the system used at the Platanares plant results in "little or no emissions," making it environmentally friendly.': Misattributes the reason. Fazal links low emissions to returning fluid to the injection well, not to the use of high-temperature geothermal fluid (which all plant types do). The causal claim is fabricated. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Since they return all geothermal fluid to the injection well, binary cycle systems — such as the one at the Platanares plant — produce "the least environmental impact," according to Fazal.': Quotes Fazal accurately, correctly identifies the mechanism (returning fluid), names the advantage (least environmental impact), and ties it to Platanares as a binary cycle plant. Hits all three goals. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Platanares plant uses a binary cycle system to produce electricity; in this type of geothermal system, fluid is pumped "back to the injection well," Fazal explains.': Quotes Fazal but never states an advantage. Pumping fluid back to the injection well is mentioned as a fact with no explanation of why it matters or what benefit it provides. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
